plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name limit.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for more income and broad market exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single-name concentration.
This conservative plan aims for capital preservation by reducing overall risk and leverage.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, leading to lower expected drawdown.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its below stop status and to reduce overall leverage.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 12% to manage single-name concentration.
Buy XLF and PANW at 5% each, adding high-scoring, diversified growth opportunities.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding high-quality setups, offering a moderate risk profile.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration limit.
Sell SOXL, reallocating from a below-stop position into stronger setups.
Trim SMH to 5% to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector.
Add to CRWD, pressing into a strong uptrending name with good P&L.
Buy NVDU and PANW at 10% each, and ASML at 5%, leveraging high-scoring scanner setups.
This plan leans into leverage and concentration for aggressive growth potential.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by leveraging high-conviction setups and accepting higher concentration. Be aware of significantly increased drawdown risk, the daily decay of leveraged ETFs, and the need for strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% and QQQ to 10%, reallocating from broad index to income.
Significantly increase QQQI to 35% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Sell CRWD and SMH to exit high-beta single names and sectors.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
This plan focuses on maximizing yield and reducing drawdown for income generation.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month, prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for cash-like income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff, so it's not a savings account.
